formula_note: Low = 2,004 reported Russian federal deaths + 1,380 reported rebel deaths. Best = 2,500 federal deaths (retrospective Kramer synthesis) + 1,380 rebel deaths, rounded to 4,000. High = 5,000 to absorb likely undercount in official/self-reported figures and residual spring 2000 combat.
uncertainty_note: Combatant deaths are the weakest core numerator: official Russian reporting is contested, rebel self-reporting may also undercount, and accessible sources do not cleanly separate all pro-Moscow auxiliaries from Russian federal forces.
quality_note: Derived from contested official and rebel statements cross-checked against later synthesis.

formula_note: Low and high retain the published interval; best = midpoint of 6,500 and 10,400.
uncertainty_note: This is the strongest accessible aggregate civilian direct-death interval for the open-war phase, but it is still a secondary synthesis, not a full named-victim registry, and it does not side-allocate the aggregate fatalities.
quality_note: Best available accessible episode-scale civilian death interval; still broader and less auditable than a full victim registry.

formula_note: No usable unknown-status direct-death series identified for this episode.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status fatalities are kept separate and excluded from the core DR. This avoids silently folding unidentified victims into either civilians or combatants.
quality_note: Explicit null estimate under strict exclusion.

formula_note: Episode length = 252 days ≈ 8.28 months. Interpolate Russian federal presence from roughly 100,000 (October 1999) to 80,000 (late May 2000), then add 5,000-7,500 insurgents during the fixed-battle phase and round to broad scenario bands.
uncertainty_note: The denominator excludes the whole Russian military and counts only organized armed personnel directly operating in the theater, but month-by-month rosters are not available in the accessible sources.
quality_note: Reasonable person-month approximation, but not from a true deployment register.

formula_note: Episode length = 252 days ≈ 8.28 months. Low/best/high scenarios imply average in-theater civilian presence of roughly 423k / 543k / 688k. These are bounded by the March 2000 300k-450k in-republic estimate, the large outflow to Ingushetia, and a later October 2000 registration ceiling net of about 47k duplicates.
uncertainty_note: This is the most assumption-sensitive part of the bundle. No accessible monthly civilian-presence series exists for the full episode, so displacement and partial return must be interpolated.
quality_note: Scenario-based person-month denominator grounded in humanitarian population anchors.

formula_note: Computed as (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) ÷ (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months). Low uses the combination most favorable to a lower DR; high uses the combination most favorable to a higher DR.; input_claim_ids inferred from episode mil_deaths_direct/civ_deaths_direct provenance during ingest cleanup
uncertainty_note: Because both combatant deaths and civilian exposure are uncertain, the interval is intentionally wide. Under strict exclusion of unknown-status deaths, the best estimate is about 2.66, with plausible bounds from about 1.34 to 5.85.
quality_note: Person-month DR is estimable, but only with broad scenario bounds.

formula_note: Incident-level evidence exists, but no episode-complete u15 direct-death series was found in accessible sources.
uncertainty_note: The bundle retains only incident-level child-death evidence for this episode and leaves the episode aggregate null.
quality_note: Auxiliary metric retrieved but not aggregable to the full episode.

formula_note: Incident-level evidence exists, but no episode-complete women direct-death series was found in accessible sources.
uncertainty_note: The bundle retains only incident-level female-death evidence for this episode and leaves the episode aggregate null.
quality_note: Auxiliary metric retrieved but not aggregable to the full episode.
